model_narrative: A rural production brigade plans its yearly farming operation. It decides how much paddy land to plant to each crop rotation at each fertilization level, how much upland to plant to each upland crop, how many pigs and shoats to raise, how much grain feed to mix, how much of each fertilizer to prepare and apply, how much of each purchasable commodity to buy in, and how much of each commodity to sell both under quota and above quota. From these decisions it accumulates a total cash cost and a total income. The objective is to maximize the brigade's income.

sets.name: ca
sets.doc: all commodities tracked by the brigade, covering crops, grains, animal products, fertilizers, and other materials

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the crops, a subset of all commodities

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grains, a subset of crops that can be used in feed mixing

sets.name: cu
sets.doc: the upland crops, the commodities that can be grown on upland fields

sets.name: cp
sets.doc: the commodities that may be purchased from outside the brigade

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: the commodities that may be sold, either under quota or above quota

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the crop sequences, the multi-crop rotation patterns that can be planted on paddy land over a year

sets.name: sh
sets.doc: the crop sequences that can be run at a higher fertilizer application level

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the fertilizers and composting materials applied to the land

sets.name: nh
sets.doc: the nutrients plus humus

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the plant nutrients

sets.name: en
sets.doc: the effective nutrient and humus categories that the soil balance is tracked in

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the fertilization intensity levels at which a crop sequence can be planted

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the pig raising activities

sets.name: ss
sets.doc: the feasible pairs of crop sequence and fertilization level; only these sequence-intensity combinations may be planted, so every sum over paddy cropping activities ranges over exactly these pairs

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the labor time periods within the year

params.name: paddy
params.doc: the area of paddy land available, in mu

params.name: upland
params.doc: the area of upland available, in mu

params.name: grainquota
params.doc: the grain sales quota that must be met, in tons

params.name: lsup
params.doc: the labor supply per period, in gongs per day

params.name: mcp
params.doc: the multi cropping pattern coefficients giving, for each crop sequence, the area share of each crop in that sequence, in mu

params.name: yield_
params.doc: paddy land crop yields, in tons per mu; the underlying attribute is named yield_ because yield is a reserved word

params.name: yieldu
params.doc: upland crop yields, in tons per mu

params.name: cxcrop
params.doc: the cash cost of each paddy crop sequence, in yuan per mu

params.name: aqsprice
params.doc: the above-quota sales price of each commodity, in yuan per ton

params.name: purdata
params.doc: purchase data indexed by commodity and attribute; the price attribute gives the purchase price in yuan per ton and the quantity attribute gives the purchase limit

params.name: pigio
params.doc: the input-output coefficients of pig raising activities; positive entries are outputs produced and negative entries are inputs consumed, per ton of activity

params.name: cxpig
params.doc: the cash cost of each pig raising activity, in yuan per ton

params.name: gio
params.doc: the grain feed mixing recipe coefficients giving, per ton of mixed feed, the commodities produced or consumed

params.name: lab
params.doc: the labor required by each crop sequence in each time period, in gongs per mu

params.name: labj
params.doc: the labor adjustment coefficient for each period; the available labor in a period is the base supply scaled up by one plus this coefficient

params.name: enc
params.doc: the effective nutrient content of each fertilizer for each effective nutrient category, as a percentage by weight

params.name: sreq
params.doc: the effective nutrient and humus requirement of each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: nup
params.doc: the effective nutrient requirement of each upland crop, per mu of upland cropping

params.name: cxfert
params.doc: the cash cost of each fertilizer, in yuan per ton

params.name: syield
params.doc: the output of each commodity from each paddy crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: crec
params.doc: the composting and fertilizing recipe coefficients giving the amount of each commodity consumed per unit of each fertilizer prepared

params.name: chemnall
params.doc: chemical nitrogen allocation coefficients per ton of sold commodity; the qsa attribute applies to quota sales and the aqsa attribute applies to above-quota sales

params.name: schem
params.doc: the chemical fertilizer allocation generated per mu of each paddy crop sequence

params.name: cdata
params.doc: crop data indexed by commodity and attribute; the proc-price attribute gives the procurement (quota) sales price in yuan per ton and the quota-sale attribute gives the required minimum quota sales

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: the paddy land area planted to each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in mu; defined only over the feasible sequence-level pairs

vars.name: xupland
vars.doc: the upland area planted to each upland crop, in mu

vars.name: xpig
vars.doc: the level of each pig raising activity, in tons

vars.name: xfeed
vars.doc: the amount of grain feed mixed for each grain, in tons

vars.name: xfert
vars.doc: the amount of each fertilizer prepared and applied, in kg

vars.name: purchase
vars.doc: the amount of each purchasable commodity bought from outside, in tons

vars.name: sales
vars.doc: the quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: aqsales
vars.doc: the above-quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: ccost
vars.doc: the total cash cost of the brigade, in yuan

vars.name: income
vars.doc: the total brigade income, in yuan

description: For each commodity the brigade cannot use more than it makes available. The amount of a commodity made available comes from what the paddy crop sequences yield, what the pig raising activities net out, what is bought in when the commodity is purchasable, what the grain feed mixing produces, and the upland harvest when the commodity is an upland crop. The amount used up is what is sold under quota and above quota when the commodity is saleable, plus what is consumed in preparing the fertilizers. For every commodity the available amount must be at least the amount used up.
expected_pyomo:
def mb_rule(model, ca):
    produced = (
        sum(model.syield[ca, s_idx, f_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + sum(model.pigio[ca, p_idx] * model.xpig[p_idx] for p_idx in model.p)
        + (model.purchase[ca] if ca in model.cp else 0)
        + sum(model.gio[ca, g_idx] * model.xfeed[g_idx] for g_idx in model.g)
        + (model.yieldu[ca] * model.xupland[ca] if ca in model.cu else 0)
    )
    consumed = (
        ((model.sales[ca] + model.aqsales[ca]) if ca in model.cs else 0)
        + sum(model.crec[ca, cf_idx] * model.xfert[cf_idx] for cf_idx in model.cf)
    )
    return produced >= consumed
model.mb = Constraint(model.ca, rule=mb_rule)

description: In every labor time period the work demanded by the planted paddy crop sequences cannot exceed the labor available that period. The labor available is the base labor supply scaled up by the period's adjustment factor. Apply this for each time period.

description: For each effective nutrient and humus category the soil's needs must be covered by what the fertilizers supply. The need comes from the requirements of the planted paddy crop sequences together with the requirements of the upland crops, and the supply comes from the effective nutrient content of the fertilizers prepared. For every category the requirement must not exceed the supply. Note the upland requirement enters at one tenth of one percent scale and the fertilizer supply at one percent scale, matching the units the coefficients are tabulated in.
expected_pyomo:
def fert_rule(model, en):
    required = (
        sum(model.sreq[s_idx, en, f_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + 0.001 * sum(model.nup[cu_idx, en] * model.xupland[cu_idx] for cu_idx in model.cu)
    )
    supplied = 0.01 * sum(model.enc[cf_idx, en] * model.xfert[cf_idx] for cf_idx in model.cf)
    return required <= supplied
model.fert = Constraint(model.en, rule=fert_rule)

description: The chemical nitrogen generated within the brigade must be enough to cover the chemical nitrogen bought in. Chemical nitrogen is generated by the planted paddy crop sequences and by the quota and above-quota sales of the saleable commodities. The chemical nitrogen bought in is the amount of ammonium bicarbonate purchased. The generated amount must be at least the purchased amount.
expected_pyomo:
def chemn_rule(model):
    generated = (
        sum(model.schem[s_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + sum(model.chemnall[cs_idx, 'qsa'] * model.sales[cs_idx] + model.chemnall[cs_idx, 'aqsa'] * model.aqsales[cs_idx] for cs_idx in model.cs)
    )
    return generated >= model.purchase['amm-bi']
model.chemn = Constraint(rule=chemn_rule)

params.doc: the cash cost of each paddy crop sequence, in yuan per mu

params.name: aqsprice
params.doc: the above-quota sales price of each commodity, in yuan per ton

params.name: purdata
params.doc: purchase data indexed by commodity and attribute; the price attribute gives the purchase price in yuan per ton and the quantity attribute gives the purchase limit

params.name: pigio
params.doc: the input-output coefficients of pig raising activities; positive entries are outputs produced and negative entries are inputs consumed, per ton of activity

params.name: cxpig
params.doc: the cash cost of each pig raising activity, in yuan per ton

params.name: gio
params.doc: the grain feed mixing recipe coefficients giving, per ton of mixed feed, the commodities produced or consumed

params.name: lab
params.doc: the labor required by each crop sequence in each time period, in gongs per mu

params.name: labj
params.doc: the labor adjustment coefficient for each period; the available labor in a period is the base supply scaled up by one plus this coefficient

params.name: enc
params.doc: the effective nutrient content of each fertilizer for each effective nutrient category, as a percentage by weight

params.name: sreq
params.doc: the effective nutrient and humus requirement of each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: nup
params.doc: the effective nutrient requirement of each upland crop, per mu of upland cropping

params.name: cxfert
params.doc: the cash cost of each fertilizer, in yuan per ton

params.name: syield
params.doc: the output of each commodity from each paddy crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: crec
params.doc: the composting and fertilizing recipe coefficients giving the amount of each commodity consumed per unit of each fertilizer prepared

params.name: chemnall
params.doc: chemical nitrogen allocation coefficients per ton of sold commodity; the qsa attribute applies to quota sales and the aqsa attribute applies to above-quota sales

params.name: schem
params.doc: the chemical fertilizer allocation generated per mu of each paddy crop sequence

params.name: cdata
params.doc: crop data indexed by commodity and attribute; the proc-price attribute gives the procurement (quota) sales price in yuan per ton and the quota-sale attribute gives the required minimum quota sales

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: the paddy land area planted to each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in mu; defined only over the feasible sequence-level pairs

vars.name: xupland
vars.doc: the upland area planted to each upland crop, in mu

vars.name: xpig
vars.doc: the level of each pig raising activity, in tons

vars.name: xfeed
vars.doc: the amount of grain feed mixed for each grain, in tons

vars.name: xfert
vars.doc: the amount of each fertilizer prepared and applied, in kg

vars.name: purchase
vars.doc: the amount of each purchasable commodity bought from outside, in tons

vars.name: sales
vars.doc: the quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: aqsales
vars.doc: the above-quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: ccost
vars.doc: the total cash cost of the brigade, in yuan

vars.name: income
vars.doc: the total brigade income, in yuan

description: The total paddy land planted across all crop sequences cannot exceed the paddy land available.

model_narrative: A rural production brigade plans its yearly farming operation. It decides how much paddy land to plant to each crop rotation at each fertilization level, how much upland to plant to each upland crop, how many pigs and shoats to raise, how much grain feed to mix, how much of each fertilizer to prepare and apply, how much of each purchasable commodity to buy in, and how much of each commodity to sell both under quota and above quota. From these decisions it accumulates a total cash cost and a total income. The objective is to maximize the brigade's income.

sets.name: ca
sets.doc: all commodities tracked by the brigade, covering crops, grains, animal products, fertilizers, and other materials

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the crops, a subset of all commodities

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grains, a subset of crops that can be used in feed mixing

sets.name: cu
sets.doc: the upland crops, the commodities that can be grown on upland fields

sets.name: cp
sets.doc: the commodities that may be purchased from outside the brigade

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: the commodities that may be sold, either under quota or above quota

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the crop sequences, the multi-crop rotation patterns that can be planted on paddy land over a year

sets.name: sh
sets.doc: the crop sequences that can be run at a higher fertilizer application level

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the fertilizers and composting materials applied to the land

sets.name: nh
sets.doc: the nutrients plus humus

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the plant nutrients

sets.name: en
sets.doc: the effective nutrient and humus categories that the soil balance is tracked in

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the fertilization intensity levels at which a crop sequence can be planted

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the pig raising activities

sets.name: ss
sets.doc: the feasible pairs of crop sequence and fertilization level; only these sequence-intensity combinations may be planted, so every sum over paddy cropping activities ranges over exactly these pairs

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the labor time periods within the year

description: Each crop sequence both consumes green manure seeds and produces green manure. Across all planted paddy crop sequences, the green manure seeds available net of what is taken to grow the green manure must be nonnegative, where for each sequence sixteen percent of the green manure output is charged back against its seed contribution. In other words the net seed balance over the whole paddy plan must be at least zero.

model_narrative: A rural production brigade plans its yearly farming operation. It decides how much paddy land to plant to each crop rotation at each fertilization level, how much upland to plant to each upland crop, how many pigs and shoats to raise, how much grain feed to mix, how much of each fertilizer to prepare and apply, how much of each purchasable commodity to buy in, and how much of each commodity to sell both under quota and above quota. From these decisions it accumulates a total cash cost and a total income. The objective is to maximize the brigade's income.

description: The total cash cost is defined as the sum of the cash costs of the planted paddy crop sequences, the cash costs of the pig raising activities, the cash costs of the fertilizers prepared, and the purchase costs of the commodities bought in. Set the cash cost variable equal to this total.

model_narrative: A rural production brigade plans its yearly farming operation. It decides how much paddy land to plant to each crop rotation at each fertilization level, how much upland to plant to each upland crop, how many pigs and shoats to raise, how much grain feed to mix, how much of each fertilizer to prepare and apply, how much of each purchasable commodity to buy in, and how much of each commodity to sell both under quota and above quota. From these decisions it accumulates a total cash cost and a total income. The objective is to maximize the brigade's income.

params.doc: the multi cropping pattern coefficients giving, for each crop sequence, the area share of each crop in that sequence, in mu

params.name: yield_
params.doc: paddy land crop yields, in tons per mu; the underlying attribute is named yield_ because yield is a reserved word

params.name: yieldu
params.doc: upland crop yields, in tons per mu

params.name: cxcrop
params.doc: the cash cost of each paddy crop sequence, in yuan per mu

params.name: aqsprice
params.doc: the above-quota sales price of each commodity, in yuan per ton

params.name: purdata
params.doc: purchase data indexed by commodity and attribute; the price attribute gives the purchase price in yuan per ton and the quantity attribute gives the purchase limit

params.name: pigio
params.doc: the input-output coefficients of pig raising activities; positive entries are outputs produced and negative entries are inputs consumed, per ton of activity

params.name: cxpig
params.doc: the cash cost of each pig raising activity, in yuan per ton

params.name: gio
params.doc: the grain feed mixing recipe coefficients giving, per ton of mixed feed, the commodities produced or consumed

params.name: lab
params.doc: the labor required by each crop sequence in each time period, in gongs per mu

params.name: labj
params.doc: the labor adjustment coefficient for each period; the available labor in a period is the base supply scaled up by one plus this coefficient

params.name: enc
params.doc: the effective nutrient content of each fertilizer for each effective nutrient category, as a percentage by weight

params.name: sreq
params.doc: the effective nutrient and humus requirement of each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: nup
params.doc: the effective nutrient requirement of each upland crop, per mu of upland cropping

params.name: cxfert
params.doc: the cash cost of each fertilizer, in yuan per ton

params.name: syield
params.doc: the output of each commodity from each paddy crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: crec
params.doc: the composting and fertilizing recipe coefficients giving the amount of each commodity consumed per unit of each fertilizer prepared

params.name: chemnall
params.doc: chemical nitrogen allocation coefficients per ton of sold commodity; the qsa attribute applies to quota sales and the aqsa attribute applies to above-quota sales

params.name: schem
params.doc: the chemical fertilizer allocation generated per mu of each paddy crop sequence

params.name: cdata
params.doc: crop data indexed by commodity and attribute; the proc-price attribute gives the procurement (quota) sales price in yuan per ton and the quota-sale attribute gives the required minimum quota sales

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: the paddy land area planted to each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in mu; defined only over the feasible sequence-level pairs

vars.name: xupland
vars.doc: the upland area planted to each upland crop, in mu

vars.name: xpig
vars.doc: the level of each pig raising activity, in tons

vars.name: xfeed
vars.doc: the amount of grain feed mixed for each grain, in tons

vars.name: xfert
vars.doc: the amount of each fertilizer prepared and applied, in kg

vars.name: purchase
vars.doc: the amount of each purchasable commodity bought from outside, in tons

vars.name: sales
vars.doc: the quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: aqsales
vars.doc: the above-quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: ccost
vars.doc: the total cash cost of the brigade, in yuan

vars.name: income
vars.doc: the total brigade income, in yuan

description: The brigade's income is defined as its sales revenue minus its cash cost. Sales revenue values the quota sales of each saleable commodity at its procurement price and the above-quota sales at its above-quota price, summed over all saleable commodities. Set the income variable equal to this revenue less the total cash cost.

model_narrative: A rural production brigade plans its yearly farming operation. It decides how much paddy land to plant to each crop rotation at each fertilization level, how much upland to plant to each upland crop, how many pigs and shoats to raise, how much grain feed to mix, how much of each fertilizer to prepare and apply, how much of each purchasable commodity to buy in, and how much of each commodity to sell both under quota and above quota. From these decisions it accumulates a total cash cost and a total income. The objective is to maximize the brigade's income.

sets.name: ca
sets.doc: all commodities tracked by the brigade, covering crops, grains, animal products, fertilizers, and other materials

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the crops, a subset of all commodities

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grains, a subset of crops that can be used in feed mixing

sets.name: cu
sets.doc: the upland crops, the commodities that can be grown on upland fields

sets.name: cp
sets.doc: the commodities that may be purchased from outside the brigade

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: the commodities that may be sold, either under quota or above quota

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the crop sequences, the multi-crop rotation patterns that can be planted on paddy land over a year

sets.name: sh
sets.doc: the crop sequences that can be run at a higher fertilizer application level

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the fertilizers and composting materials applied to the land

sets.name: nh
sets.doc: the nutrients plus humus

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the plant nutrients

sets.name: en
sets.doc: the effective nutrient and humus categories that the soil balance is tracked in

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the fertilization intensity levels at which a crop sequence can be planted

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the pig raising activities

sets.name: ss
sets.doc: the feasible pairs of crop sequence and fertilization level; only these sequence-intensity combinations may be planted, so every sum over paddy cropping activities ranges over exactly these pairs

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the labor time periods within the year

params.name: paddy
params.doc: the area of paddy land available, in mu

params.name: upland
params.doc: the area of upland available, in mu

params.name: grainquota
params.doc: the grain sales quota that must be met, in tons

params.name: lsup
params.doc: the labor supply per period, in gongs per day

params.name: mcp
params.doc: the multi cropping pattern coefficients giving, for each crop sequence, the area share of each crop in that sequence, in mu

params.name: yield_
params.doc: paddy land crop yields, in tons per mu; the underlying attribute is named yield_ because yield is a reserved word

params.name: yieldu
params.doc: upland crop yields, in tons per mu

params.name: cxcrop

params.name: aqsprice

params.name: purdata

params.name: pigio

params.name: cxpig

params.name: gio
params.doc: the grain feed mixing recipe coefficients giving, per ton of mixed feed, the commodities produced or consumed

params.name: lab

params.name: labj

params.name: enc

params.name: sreq
params.doc: the effective nutrient and humus requirement of each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in tons per mu

params.name: nup
params.doc: the effective nutrient requirement of each upland crop, per mu of upland cropping

params.name: cxfert
params.doc: the cash cost of each fertilizer, in yuan per ton

params.name: syield

params.name: crec
params.doc: the composting and fertilizing recipe coefficients giving the amount of each commodity consumed per unit of each fertilizer prepared

params.name: chemnall
params.doc: chemical nitrogen allocation coefficients per ton of sold commodity; the qsa attribute applies to quota sales and the aqsa attribute applies to above-quota sales

params.name: schem
params.doc: the chemical fertilizer allocation generated per mu of each paddy crop sequence

params.name: cdata
params.doc: crop data indexed by commodity and attribute; the proc-price attribute gives the procurement (quota) sales price in yuan per ton and the quota-sale attribute gives the required minimum quota sales

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: the paddy land area planted to each crop sequence at each fertilization level, in mu; defined only over the feasible sequence-level pairs

vars.name: xupland
vars.doc: the upland area planted to each upland crop, in mu

vars.name: xpig
vars.doc: the level of each pig raising activity, in tons

vars.name: xfeed
vars.doc: the amount of grain feed mixed for each grain, in tons

vars.name: xfert
vars.doc: the amount of each fertilizer prepared and applied, in kg

vars.name: purchase
vars.doc: the amount of each purchasable commodity bought from outside, in tons

vars.name: sales
vars.doc: the quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: aqsales
vars.doc: the above-quota sales of each commodity, in tons

vars.name: ccost
vars.doc: the total cash cost of the brigade, in yuan

vars.name: income
vars.doc: the total brigade income, in yuan

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, for each commodity the brigade cannot use more than it makes available. The amount of a commodity made available comes from what the paddy crop sequences yield, what the pig raising activities net out, what is bought in when the commodity is purchasable, what the grain feed mixing produces, and the upland harvest when the commodity is an upland crop. The amount used up is what is sold under quota and above quota when the commodity is saleable, plus what is consumed in preparing the fertilizers. For every commodity the available amount must be at least the amount used up. Second, in every labor time period the work demanded by the planted paddy crop sequences cannot exceed the labor available that period. The labor available is the base labor supply scaled up by the period's adjustment factor. Apply this for each time period. Third, for each effective nutrient and humus category the soil's needs must be covered by what the fertilizers supply. The need comes from the requirements of the planted paddy crop sequences together with the requirements of the upland crops, and the supply comes from the effective nutrient content of the fertilizers prepared. For every category the requirement must not exceed the supply. Note the upland requirement enters at one tenth of one percent scale and the fertilizer supply at one percent scale, matching the units the coefficients are tabulated in. Fourth, the chemical nitrogen generated within the brigade must be enough to cover the chemical nitrogen bought in. Chemical nitrogen is generated by the planted paddy crop sequences and by the quota and above-quota sales of the saleable commodities. The chemical nitrogen bought in is the amount of ammonium bicarbonate purchased. The generated amount must be at least the purchased amount. Fifth, the total paddy land planted across all crop sequences cannot exceed the paddy land available. Sixth, the total upland planted across all upland crops cannot exceed the upland available. Seventh, each crop sequence both consumes green manure seeds and produces green manure. Across all planted paddy crop sequences, the green manure seeds available net of what is taken to grow the green manure must be nonnegative, where for each sequence sixteen percent of the green manure output is charged back against its seed contribution. In other words the net seed balance over the whole paddy plan must be at least zero. Eighth, the grain sold under quota, summed over all grains, must meet at least the grain sales quota. Ninth, the total cash cost is defined as the sum of the cash costs of the planted paddy crop sequences, the cash costs of the pig raising activities, the cash costs of the fertilizers prepared, and the purchase costs of the commodities bought in. Set the cash cost variable equal to this total. Finally, the brigade's income is defined as its sales revenue minus its cash cost. Sales revenue values the quota sales of each saleable commodity at its procurement price and the above-quota sales at its above-quota price, summed over all saleable commodities. Set the income variable equal to this revenue less the total cash cost.
expected_pyomo:
def mb_rule(model, ca):
    produced = (
        sum(model.syield[ca, s_idx, f_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + sum(model.pigio[ca, p_idx] * model.xpig[p_idx] for p_idx in model.p)
        + (model.purchase[ca] if ca in model.cp else 0)
        + sum(model.gio[ca, g_idx] * model.xfeed[g_idx] for g_idx in model.g)
        + (model.yieldu[ca] * model.xupland[ca] if ca in model.cu else 0)
    )
    consumed = (
        ((model.sales[ca] + model.aqsales[ca]) if ca in model.cs else 0)
        + sum(model.crec[ca, cf_idx] * model.xfert[cf_idx] for cf_idx in model.cf)
    )
    return produced >= consumed
model.mb = Constraint(model.ca, rule=mb_rule)
def labor_rule(model, t):
    return sum(model.lab[t, s_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss) <= (1 + model.labj[t]) * model.lsup
model.labor = Constraint(model.t, rule=labor_rule)
def fert_rule(model, en):
    required = (
        sum(model.sreq[s_idx, en, f_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + 0.001 * sum(model.nup[cu_idx, en] * model.xupland[cu_idx] for cu_idx in model.cu)
    )
    supplied = 0.01 * sum(model.enc[cf_idx, en] * model.xfert[cf_idx] for cf_idx in model.cf)
    return required <= supplied
model.fert = Constraint(model.en, rule=fert_rule)
def chemn_rule(model):
    generated = (
        sum(model.schem[s_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + sum(model.chemnall[cs_idx, 'qsa'] * model.sales[cs_idx] + model.chemnall[cs_idx, 'aqsa'] * model.aqsales[cs_idx] for cs_idx in model.cs)
    )
    return generated >= model.purchase['amm-bi']
model.chemn = Constraint(rule=chemn_rule)
def landp_rule(model):
    return sum(model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss) <= model.paddy
model.landp = Constraint(rule=landp_rule)
def landu_rule(model):
    return sum(model.xupland[cu_idx] for cu_idx in model.cu) <= model.upland
model.landu = Constraint(rule=landu_rule)
def gmseed_rule(model):
    return sum(((1 - 0.16) * model.mcp[s_idx, 'gm-seeds'] - 0.16 * model.mcp[s_idx, 'g-manure']) * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss) >= 0
model.gmseed = Constraint(rule=gmseed_rule)
def grainq_rule(model):
    return sum(model.sales[g_idx] for g_idx in model.g) >= model.grainquota
model.grainq = Constraint(rule=grainq_rule)
def cdef_rule(model):
    return model.ccost == (
        sum(model.cxcrop[s_idx] * model.xcrop[s_idx, f_idx] for (s_idx, f_idx) in model.ss)
        + sum(model.cxpig[p_idx] * model.xpig[p_idx] for p_idx in model.p)
        + sum(model.cxfert[cf_idx] * model.xfert[cf_idx] for cf_idx in model.cf)
        + sum(model.purdata[cp_idx, 'price'] * model.purchase[cp_idx] for cp_idx in model.cp)
    )
model.cdef = Constraint(rule=cdef_rule)
def incdef_rule(model):
    return model.income == sum(model.cdata[cs_idx, 'proc-price'] * model.sales[cs_idx] + model.aqsprice[cs_idx] * model.aqsales[cs_idx] for cs_idx in model.cs) - model.ccost
model.incdef = Constraint(rule=incdef_rule)
